title: NAT Rule Utilization
spl:
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:nat_stats" earliest=-1h
| eval used_pct=round(100*active_sessions/session_limit,1)
| where used_pct>85
| table edge_node, rule_id, active_sessions, session_limit, used_pct
| sort -used_pct
description: Tracks NAT session and port allocation per rule ‚Äî prevents exhaustion on busy Edge gateways.
value: Tracks NAT session and port allocation per rule ‚Äî prevents exhaustion on busy Edge gateways.
implementation: Session limits depend on Edge form factor; load from capacity sheet via lookup. Alert at 85%.
visualization: Bar chart (NAT % by rule), Table (top consumers), Gauge.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `vmware_nsx_addon`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `vmware:nsx:nat_stats`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Session limits depend on Edge form factor; load from capacity sheet via lookup. Alert at 85%.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:nat_stats" earliest=-1h
| eval used_pct=round(100*active_sessions/session_limit,1)
| where used_pct>85
| table edge_node, rule_id, active_sessions, session_limit, used_pct
| sort -used_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**NAT Rule Utilization** ‚Äî Tracks NAT session and port allocation per rule ‚Äî prevents exhaustion on busy Edge gateways.

Documented **Data sources**: `vmware:nsx:nat_stats`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `vmware_nsx_addon`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vmware; **sourcetype**: vmware:nsx:nat_stats. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=vmware, sourcetype="vmware:nsx:nat_stats", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **used_pct** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where used_pct>85` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **NAT Rule Utilization**): table edge_node, rule_id, active_sessions, session_limit, used_pct
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (NAT % by rule), Table (top consumers), Gauge.
grandmaExplanation: We track session and port allocation per rule ‚Äî prevents exhaustion on busy Edge gateways ‚Äî so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
